name: openai
description: Query OpenAI models, generate text responses, embeddings, and images via the OpenAI API

tools.name: List Models
tools.description: List the models available to the API key

historicalSummary.omitted: model capability details and provider-specific payload fields

tools.name: Get Model
tools.description: Get details about a specific model

model.description: Model ID, e.g. gpt-4o or o4-mini

tools.name: Create Response
tools.description: Generate a model response using the OpenAI Responses API

model.description: Model ID to use, e.g. gpt-4o

input.description: Text input or prompt for the model

tools.name: Create Chat Completion
tools.description: Generate a chat completion from a list of messages

model.description: Model ID to use, e.g. gpt-4o

messages.description: Chat messages, each with role (system|user|assistant) and content

tools.name: Create Embedding
tools.description: Create an embedding vector for input text

dimensions.description: Number of dimensions for the output embedding (supported models only)

tools.name: Generate Image
tools.description: Generate an image from a text prompt

model.description: Image model ID, e.g. gpt-image-1 or dall-e-3

prompt.description: Text description of the desired image

tools.name: Upload File
tools.description: Upload a file for use with assistants, batch, fine-tune, vision, user_data, or evals

purpose.description: Intended purpose of the file: assistants, batch, fine-tune, vision, user_data, or evals

file.description: Base64-encoded file content, sent decoded as the binary multipart 'file' part

filename.description: Filename for the uploaded file part, e.g. training.jsonl; the extension helps OpenAI validate the file type

tools.name: List Files
tools.description: List files uploaded to the organization, optionally filtered by purpose

purpose.description: Only return files with the given purpose, e.g. assistants or batch

order.description: Sort order by created_at: asc or desc

after.description: Pagination cursor: object ID from the previous page

tools.name: Get File
tools.description: Get metadata about an uploaded file by ID

file_id.description: ID of the file, e.g. file-abc123

tools.name: Delete File
tools.description: Delete an uploaded file and remove it from all vector stores

file_id.description: ID of the file to delete, e.g. file-abc123

tools.name: Get Completions Usage
tools.description: Get token usage for completions across the organization, bucketed over time. Requires an organization admin API key (created under Settings → Organization → Admin keys) set as OPENAI_API_KEY — a regular project key returns 401

start_time.description: Start time as a Unix timestamp in seconds, inclusive

end_time.description: End time as a Unix timestamp in seconds, exclusive

group_by.description: Group usage by fields: project_id, user_id, api_key_id, model, batch, service_tier

limit.description: Number of buckets to return (max 31 for 1d, 168 for 1h, 1440 for 1m)

page.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response's next_page

tools.name: Get Costs
tools.description: Get daily spend (in USD) for the organization, bucketed by day. Requires an organization admin API key (created under Settings → Organization → Admin keys) set as OPENAI_API_KEY — a regular project key returns 401

start_time.description: Start time as a Unix timestamp in seconds, inclusive

end_time.description: End time as a Unix timestamp in seconds, exclusive

group_by.description: Group costs by fields: project_id, line_item, api_key_id

limit.description: Number of daily buckets to return (1-180)

page.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response's next_page

tools.name: Get File Content
tools.description: Download the raw contents of an uploaded file by ID — required to read batch output files and other generated artifacts

file_id.description: ID of the file to download, e.g. file-abc123

tools.name: Get Embeddings Usage
tools.description: Get token usage for embeddings across the organization, bucketed over time. Requires an organization admin API key (created under Settings → Organization → Admin keys) set as OPENAI_API_KEY — a regular project key returns 401

start_time.description: Start time as a Unix timestamp in seconds, inclusive

end_time.description: End time as a Unix timestamp in seconds, exclusive

group_by.description: Group usage by fields: project_id, user_id, api_key_id, model

models.description: Return only usage for these models

limit.description: Number of buckets to return (max 31 for 1d, 168 for 1h, 1440 for 1m)

page.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response's next_page

prompts.title: List available models
prompts.prompt: List the OpenAI models available to my API key and summarize what each is best for.

prompts.title: Generate a response
prompts.prompt: Use OpenAI to generate a response to a prompt I provide.

SETUP_GUIDE.title: OpenAI API Setup

steps.title: Create an OpenAI account
steps.description: Go to https://platform.openai.com and sign up or sign in. New accounts may need to add billing details under Settings → Billing before API calls succeed.

steps.title: Create an API key
steps.description: Open https://platform.openai.com/api-keys, click 'Create new secret key', give it a descriptive name (e.g. 'Veryfront Integration'), and select the project it belongs to.

steps.title: Copy and store the key
steps.description: Copy the key immediately — it is only shown once. Add it to your .env file as OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...

steps.title: Verify access
steps.description: Run the List Models tool to confirm the key works. If you get a 401, check the key; if a 429, check your billing/quota.

notes: Use project-scoped API keys to limit blast radius; you can revoke keys at any time from the dashboard
API usage is billed per token — set usage limits under Settings → Limits

The Get Completions Usage and Get Costs tools call the organization usage API, which only accepts admin API keys created at https://platform.openai.com/settings/organization/admin-keys — set one as OPENAI_API_KEY to use them
The Upload File tool sends multipart/form-data with a binary file part; pass the file content base64-encoded
